# Transcrição: 1. Purpose of the Greeting

- **Fonte:** 1. Purpose of the Greeting
- **Duração:** 04:11
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:00]

You know the first seconds of meeting a customer, those first moments, can never be changed. You have to be prepared here and you have to do it right. This is a vital and critical step. Greeting by Merriam Webster Dictionary means to address with expression of kind wishes upon meeting or upon derival. So you're trying to make people feel welcomed. The purpose of the greeting is 1. Make people feel welcomed. Hey come into my home, you know, come into my office. Make people feel welcome. Number two, you want to introduce yourself and make yourself known to that customer. Number three, put the buyer at ease. Make them at ease so they start dropping their guard. Number four, to get on common ground. You know what do we have in common with one another and we'll talk more about each of these. Number five, very, very important and not often talked about by other trainers is you want to differentiate yourself from others. I mean differentiate yourself from others that they will meet later or they have already met. Okay and you want to also differentiate from any experiences people have had in the past with your product, your service, your appointment, the reason you're there. Number six, you want to get the buyer to turn control over to you. Now this word control is very, very confused by people but you actually want to have people turn control over to you, not take it from them and I'll talk more about that as well. Look this program on greeting is the foundation for the rest of every step you will take and it is a step that you must, you must continue to perfect throughout your career. This is not something you learn once and move away from. The first purpose here is welcome. Hey my name is Grant. See that can be learned in a second right? How easy is it? Alright? Hey welcome my name is Grant. Can you do it? Try it now. See it's the rest of the greeting that's going to make you a pro. Anyone can say hey my name is Grant. What we want to do is take everything else that you're saying right now and replace it with this. Welcome my name is. Look after that if you can't build trust you're not going to put them at ease. Okay so that's what we want to build on. So get that first greeting down. If you can't put people at ease you're going to struggle with getting on common ground. If you don't differentiate yourself you'll never control the process. So we want to welcome, make ourselves known, I want to put people at ease and I basically want to grab, differentiate myself, don't forget that and then grab control of the process by them giving me control not taking it. Look ask the people above you, executive, manager, team leader, district manager whatever. Ask them how important control is and he or she will tell you control is essential to selling.

## [03:00 - 04:11]

You will either have control or you will not have control period. There is no in between. This is something you should ask yourself during the sale. Am I in control? Do I have control of the process? Am I controlling the steps? Am I controlling how long things take? Am I controlling where we go? Am I handling objections, controlling people and they're giving me control. They want me to actually be in control not you must do this, you have to do this because people are going to resist that. This is going to come as a result of working the steps perfectly. You break these steps, you skip them, you let people talk you out of doing them. I promise you you will never ever have control and control equals a close. So in this module we will teach you how to do these things, how to handle objections that you hear, how to transition from one step to the next step. Also we're going to introduce you to the do's and don'ts about the greeting. Look the greeting is like oxygen to your lungs. Without the greeting being done correctly, without you gaining control you will not make the sale. Like this.
